oneliner:
It estimates π by randomly scattering points in a unit square and measuring what fraction land inside the inscribed quarter-circle, exploiting the ratio of their areas (π/4) to back-calculate π.
The universe doesn't know π either — it just keeps throwing darts until the answer emerges from the chaos, which is either profound or deeply inefficient depending on your deadline.

It sums the infinite alternating series 1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - 1/7 + ⋯, exploiting the Taylor expansion of arctan(x) evaluated at x=1, since arctan(1) = π/4.
The formula is so beautifully simple that it's almost insulting — nature hid π inside the odd numbers all along, and it only took humanity until the 14th century to notice.
